schema_json.description: An expert answer grounded in search results.

schema_json.title: StormAnswer

prompt:
You are a research expert being interviewed about:
RESEARCH QUESTION: What is the empirical evidence on the impact of minimum wage increases on small business employment in OECD countries since 2010?

The interviewer (Dr. A, Scientific perspective) asks:
QUESTION: What gaps remain regarding Pooled effect sizes and GRADE certainty from Scientific standpoint?

SEARCH RESULTS (your ONLY source of information):
[Source 1] Most minimum wage studies have found little or no job loss
  URL: https://www.epi.org/blog/most-minimum-wage-studies-have-found-little-or-no-job-loss/
  Content: A new review that I co-authored with Arindrajit Dube finds that most minimum wage studies find no job losses or only small disemployment effects.
[Source 2] Who's Afraid of the Minimum Wage? Measuring the Impacts on ...
  URL: https://academic.oup.com/qje/article/141/1/373/8376639
  Content: We find that independent firms finance their higher wage bills with new revenue, raising roughly 3.3% more revenue four years after the minimum wage increase.

  Content: Minimum wage increases result in small but significant increases in prices and consumption, particularly in economic sectors where firms tend to employ a large ...

  URL: https://www.jec.senate.gov/public/_cache/files/b5056335-1b0f-4751-8e3a-2739e7963738/minimum-wage-critics-lag-behind-latest-research-final.pdf

  URL: https://aier.org/article/the-economics-of-the-minimum-wage-myths-facts-and-consequences/
  Content: Empirical studies on this issue have produced mixed results, but many show that increasing the minimum wage leads to job losses, particularly ...

  URL: https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC5502230/
  Content: We provide suggestions on the use of GRADE to rate the certainty of evidence when a meta-analysis has not been performed, and instead a narrative summary of the ...
[Source 11] Assessing the certainty of the evidence in systematic reviews
  URL: https://academic.oup.com/aje/article/194/6/1681/7746729
  Content: This article introduces the GRADE approach for assessing the certainty of the evidence and describes how the assessment is done, how they are incorporated when ...
[Source 12] Chapter 14: Completing 'Summary of findings' tables and grading ...
  URL: https://www.cochrane.org/authors/handbooks-and-manuals/handbook/current/chapter-14
  Content: For systematic reviews, the GRADE approach defines the certainty of a body of evidence as the extent to which one can be confident that an estimate of ...

  URL: https://uvanl.libguides.com/c.php?g=723438&p=5319551
  Content: An effect size is a quantitative metric that captures both the direction and magnitude of a relationship between two entities. When different ...
[Source 14] Chapter 7: GRADE Criteria Determining Certainty of Evidence - CDC
  URL: https://www.cdc.gov/acip-grade-handbook/hcp/chapter-7-grade-criteria-determining-certainty-of-evidence/index.html
  Content: The GRADE approach is used to determine the certainty of evidence across the body of evidence for each outcome identified as critical or important for decision ...

  URL: https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S0895435621001086
  Content: This article provides practical principles and examples to help systematic review and health technology assessment authors make optimal choices regarding the ...

INSTRUCTIONS:
1. Provide a comprehensive, well-structured answer (200-400 words).
2. ONLY use information from the search results above. Do NOT add information not present in the sources.
3. When citing specific facts, mention which source they come from.
4. Extract 3-5 key findings as bullet points.
5. List the URLs of sources you actually used in your answer.
6. If the search results do not adequately answer the question, say so explicitly and note what information is missing.

Return a JSON object with 'answer', 'key_findings', and 'sources_used' fields.
